qtype: time duration and average speed calculation
tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 1]**

Here is part of a train timetable for a journey from London to Marseille.
\newline
All times given are in local time.
\newline
The local time in Marseille is 1 hour ahead of the local time in London.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{ll}
London & 07 19 \\
Ashford & 07 55 \\
Lyon & 13 00 \\
Avignon & 14 08 \\
Marseille & 14 46 \\
\end{tabular}

(a) Work out the total journey time from London to Marseille.
\newline
Give your answer in hours and minutes.
\newline

(i) (b) Total journey time

(c) The distance from London to Ashford is 90 km.
\newline
The local time in London is the same as the local time in Ashford.
\newline

Work out the average speed, in km/h, of the train between London and Ashford.
\newline

(d) During the journey, the train takes 35 seconds to completely cross a bridge.
\newline
The average speed of the train during this crossing is 90 km/h.
\newline
The length of the train is 95 metres.
\newline

Calculate the length, in metres, of this bridge.
\newline

(e) The fares for the train journey are shown in the table below.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{lll}
From London to Marseille & Standard fare & Premier fare \\
Adult & \$84 & \$140 \\
Child & \$60 & \$96 \\
\end{tabular}

(i) (f) For the standard fare, write the ratio adult fare : child fare in its simplest form.
\newline

(g) For an adult, find the percentage increase in the cost of the standard fare to the premier fare.
\newline

(h) For one journey from London to Marseille, the ratio

number of adults : number of children = 11 : 2.
\newline

There were 220 adults in total on this journey.
\newline
All of the children and 70\% of the adults paid the standard fare.
\newline
The remaining adults paid the premier fare.
\newline

Calculate the total of the fares paid by the adults and the children.
\newline

(i) There were $3.08 \times 10^5$ passengers that made this journey in 2018.
\newline
This was a 12\% decrease in the number of passengers that made this journey in 2017.
\newline

Find the number of passengers that made this journey in 2017.
\newline
Give your answer in standard form.
\newline

content.content:
Work out the total journey time from London to Marseille.
\newline

\newline
Total journey time

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline

\newline
The length of the train is 95 metres.
\newline

\newline

The fares for the train journey are shown in the table below.
\newline

\newline

\newline

For one journey from London to Marseille, the ratio

number of adults : number of children = 11 : 2.
\newline

There were 220 adults in total on this journey.
\newline
All of the children and 70\% of the adults paid the standard fare.
\newline

\newline

\newline

_rawBody:
\noindent\textbf{[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 1]}\par

Here is part of a train timetable for a journey from London to Marseille.
All times given are in local time.
The local time in Marseille is 1 hour ahead of the local time in London.

\begin{tabular}{ll}
London & 07 19 \\
Ashford & 07 55 \\
Lyon & 13 00 \\
Avignon & 14 08 \\
Marseille & 14 46 \\
\end{tabular}

\begin{parts}
\item Work out the total journey time from London to Marseille.
Give your answer in hours and minutes.

\begin{subparts}
\item Total journey time

\AnswerLine[][\text{h}][\text{min}]
\Marks{2}

\item The distance from London to Ashford is 90 km.
The local time in London is the same as the local time in Ashford.

Work out the average speed, in km/h, of the train between London and Ashford.

\AnswerLine[][\text{km/h}]
\Marks{3}

\item During the journey, the train takes 35 seconds to completely cross a bridge.
The average speed of the train during this crossing is 90 km/h.
The length of the train is 95 metres.

Calculate the length, in metres, of this bridge.

\AnswerLine[][\text{m}]
\Marks{4}
\end{subparts}

\item The fares for the train journey are shown in the table below.

\begin{tabular}{lll}
From London to Marseille & Standard fare & Premier fare \\
Adult & \$84 & \$140 \\
Child & \$60 & \$96 \\
\end{tabular}

\begin{subparts}
\item For the standard fare, write the ratio adult fare : child fare in its simplest form.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{1}

\item For an adult, find the percentage increase in the cost of the standard fare to the premier fare.

\AnswerLine[][\%]
\Marks{3}

\item For one journey from London to Marseille, the ratio

number of adults : number of children = 11 : 2.

There were 220 adults in total on this journey.
All of the children and 70\% of the adults paid the standard fare.
The remaining adults paid the premier fare.

Calculate the total of the fares paid by the adults and the children.

\AnswerLine[][\$]
\Marks{5}
\end{subparts}

\item There were $3.08 \times 10^5$ passengers that made this journey in 2018.
This was a 12\% decrease in the number of passengers that made this journey in 2017.

Find the number of passengers that made this journey in 2017.
Give your answer in standard form.

\AnswerLine[][]
\Marks{3}

\end{parts}

qtype: Solving linear equations, solving inequalities, simplifying expressions with indices and algebraic fractions
tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 2]**

(a) Solve.
\newline

\[5x - 17 = 7x + 3\]

(b) Find the integer values of $n$ that satisfy this inequality.
\newline

\[-7 \leq 4n \leq 8\]

(c) Simplify.
\newline

(i) (d) $a^3 \times a^6$

(e) $(5xy^2)^3$

(f) $\dfrac{27x^{12}}{64y^3} \div \dfrac{1}{p^3}$

content.content:
Solve.
\newline

\[5x - 17 = 7x + 3\]

Find the integer values of $n$ that satisfy this inequality.
\newline

\[-7 \leq 4n \leq 8\]

Simplify.
\newline
$a^3 \times a^6$

tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 3]**

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a grid with triangle A plotted on it.
\newline

(a) On the grid, draw the image of

(i) (b) triangle A after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -3 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$

(c) triangle A after a reflection in the line $y = x$.
\newline

(d) Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B.
\newline

(e) (i) (f) Find the matrix that represents an enlargement, scale factor $-2$, centre $(0, 0)$.
\newline

(g) Calculate the determinant of the matrix in part (c)(i).
\newline

triangle A after a translation by the vector $\begin{pmatrix} -3 \\ 2 \end{pmatrix}$

triangle A after a reflection in the line $y = x$.
\newline

Describe fully the single transformation that maps triangle A onto triangle B.
\newline

Find the matrix that represents an enlargement, scale factor $-2$, centre $(0, 0)$.
\newline

Calculate the determinant of the matrix in part (c)(i).
\newline

tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 4]**

(a) %\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a hemispherical bowl of radius 5.6 cm and a cylindrical tin of height 10 cm.
\newline

(i) (b) Show that the volume of the bowl is 368 cm$^3$, correct to the nearest cm$^3$.
\newline

\[\left[ The volume,  V,  of a sphere with radius  r  is  V = \frac{4}{3}\pi r^3.
\newline
 \right]\]

(c) The tin is completely full of soup.
\newline
When all the soup is poured into the empty bowl, 80\% of the volume of the bowl is filled.
\newline

Calculate the radius of the tin.
\newline

(d) %\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows a cone with radius 1.75 cm and height 6 cm.
\newline

(i) (e) Calculate the total surface area of the cone.
\newline

\[\left[ The curved surface area,  A,  of a cone with radius  r  and slant height  l  is  A = \pi rl.
\newline
 \right]\]

(f) The cone contains salt to a depth of 4.5 cm.
\newline
The top layer of the salt forms a circle that is parallel to the base of the cone.
\newline

Show that the volume of the salt inside the cone is 18.9 cm$^3$, correct to 1 decimal place.
\newline

\[\left[ The volume,  V,  of a cone with radius  r  and height  h  is  V = \frac{1}{3}\pi r^2 h.
\newline
 \right]\]

(g) The salt is removed from the cone at a constant rate of 200 mm$^3$ per second.
\newline

Calculate the time taken for the cone to be completely emptied.
\newline
Give your answer in seconds, correct to the nearest second.
\newline

\newline

\newline

qtype: Graph interpretation, solving equations graphically, estimating gradient from graph
tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 5]**

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

The diagram shows the graph of $y = f(x)$ where $f(x) = x^2 - \frac{2}{x}$, $x \neq 0$.
\newline

(a) Use the graph to find

(i) (b) $f(1)$

(c) $f(-2)$

(d) On the grid opposite, draw a suitable straight line to solve the equation
$x^2 - \frac{2}{x} + 3x = 0$ for $-3 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

(e) By drawing a suitable tangent, find an estimate of the gradient of the curve at $x = -2$.
\newline

(f) (i) (g) Complete the table for $y = g(x)$ where $g(x) = 2^{-x}$ for $-3 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{cccccccc}
$x$ & $-3$ & $-2$ & $-1$ & $0$ & $1$ & $2$ & $3$ \\
$y$ & & $2$ & $1$ & $0.5$ & & & $0.125$ \\
\end{tabular}

(h) On the grid opposite, draw the graph of $y = g(x)$.
\newline

(i) Use your graph to find the positive solution to the equation $f(x) = g(x)$.
\newline

content.content: Use the graph to find

On the grid opposite, draw a suitable straight line to solve the equation
$x^2 - \frac{2}{x} + 3x = 0$ for $-3 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

By drawing a suitable tangent, find an estimate of the gradient of the curve at $x = -2$.
\newline

Complete the table for $y = g(x)$ where $g(x) = 2^{-x}$ for $-3 \leq x \leq 3$.
\newline

On the grid opposite, draw the graph of $y = g(x)$.
\newline

Use your graph to find the positive solution to the equation $f(x) = g(x)$.
\newline

tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 6]**

The table shows the time, $t$ seconds, taken by each of 120 boys to solve a puzzle.
\newline

\begin{tabular}{cc}
Time ($t$ seconds) & Frequency \\
$20 \leq t < 30$ & 38 \\
$30 \leq t < 35$ & 27 \\
$35 \leq t < 40$ & 21 \\
$40 \leq t < 60$ & 16 \\
$60 \leq t < 100$ & 18 \\
\end{tabular}

(a) Calculate an estimate of the mean time.
\newline

(b) On the grid, complete the histogram to show the information in the frequency table.
\newline

%\begin{InsertScreenShot}
%    
%\end{InsertScreenShot}

content.content:
Calculate an estimate of the mean time.
\newline

tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 7]**

A straight line joins the points A $(-2, -3)$ and C $(1, 9)$.
\newline

(a) Find the equation of the line AC in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

(b) Calculate the acute angle between AC and the x-axis.
\newline

(c) ABCD is a kite, where AC is the longer diagonal of the kite.
\newline
B is the point $(3.5, 2)$.
\newline

(i) (d) Find the equation of the line BD in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

(e) The diagonals AC and BD intersect at $(-0.5, 3)$.
\newline

Work out the co-ordinates of D.
\newline

\newline

Calculate the acute angle between AC and the x-axis.
\newline

\newline
B is the point $(3.5, 2)$.
\newline

Find the equation of the line BD in the form $y = mx + c$.
\newline

The diagonals AC and BD intersect at $(-0.5, 3)$.
\newline

Work out the co-ordinates of D.
\newline

tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 8]**

(a) Angelo has a bag containing 3 white counters and $x$ black counters.
\newline
He takes two counters at random from the bag, without replacement.
\newline

(i) (b) Complete the following statement.
\newline

The probability that Angelo takes two black counters is $\dfrac{x(x-1)}{x+3}$.
\newline

(c) The probability that Angelo takes two black counters is $\dfrac{7}{15}$.
\newline

Show that $4x^2 - 25x - 21 = 0$.
\newline

(d) Solve by factorisation.
\newline
$4x^2 - 25x - 21 = 0$

(e) Write down the number of black counters in the bag.
\newline

(f) Esme has a bag with 5 green counters and 4 red counters.
\newline
She takes three counters at random from the bag without replacement.
\newline

Work out the probability that the three counters are all the same colour.
\newline

content.content:
Angelo has a bag containing 3 white counters and $x$ black counters.
\newline
He takes two counters at random from the bag, without replacement.
\newline

Complete the following statement.
\newline

The probability that Angelo takes two black counters is $\dfrac{x(x-1)}{x+3}$.
\newline

The probability that Angelo takes two black counters is $\dfrac{7}{15}$.
\newline

Show that $4x^2 - 25x - 21 = 0$.
\newline

Solve by factorisation.
\newline
$4x^2 - 25x - 21 = 0$

Write down the number of black counters in the bag.
\newline

Esme has a bag with 5 green counters and 4 red counters.
\newline
She takes three counters at random from the bag without replacement.
\newline

Work out the probability that the three counters are all the same colour.
\newline

qtype: Linear sequences, finding nth term, solving for n, quadratic sequences
tex:
**[2019 May/June, 0580/43, Question 10]**

(a) The sequence is 19, 15, 11, 7, \ldots

(i) (b) Write down the next two terms of the sequence.
\newline

(c) Find the $nth$ term of this sequence.
\newline

(d) Find the value of $n$ when the $nth$ term is -65.
\newline

(e) Another sequence has $nth$ term $2n^2 + 5n - 15$.
\newline

Find the difference between the 4th term and the 5th term of this sequence.
\newline

content.content: The sequence is 19, 15, 11, 7, \ldots

Write down the next two terms of the sequence.
\newline

Find the $nth$ term of this sequence.
\newline

Find the value of $n$ when the $nth$ term is -65.
\newline

Another sequence has $nth$ term $2n^2 + 5n - 15$.
\newline

Find the difference between the 4th term and the 5th term of this sequence.
\newline
